justification: Both domain rules (state transition invariant and refund precondition) are present with testable_assertions that are specific and actionable. All three architecture decisions (PATCH endpoint, OrderStateMachine, RefundService) are present with detailed implementation_notes. Acceptance criteria preserve the error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim in the first criterion. Task context is one specific, actionable paragraph. No invented information detected. Terminology alignment is present but addresses domain-to-technical mappings rather than directly reconciling Bird's 'order status' language with MJ's 'state' language — it maps phrases to code concepts instead of explicitly noting that Bird and MJ use different words for the same concept and declaring which term wins.

observations.text: Three architecture decisions instead of the minimum two — includes OrderStateMachine as its own decision with clear single-authority guidance
Acceptance criteria include three scenarios covering both invariants plus the happy path, with Given/When/Then structure and ORDER_STATE_INVALID preserved verbatim
Open questions are well-chosen and demonstrate synthesis awareness — they identify genuine gaps between Bird and MJ's inputs rather than inventing answers
Task context is concrete and actionable, naming specific components and their relationships
Notes for Shaq section adds useful implementation prioritization without inventing new requirements

Terminology alignment maps domain phrases to technical implementations but does not directly address that Bird says 'order status' while MJ says 'state' — the rubric specifically expected Magic to confirm these refer to the same concept and clarify which term is canonical

The 422 status code and structured error body guidance in architecture_guidance may go beyond what was in MJ's original input — borderline invented detail, though it is a reasonable inference from PATCH endpoint design
